ChatCompletions_Create_2024Feb15Preview.displayName: Creates a completion for the chat message

parameters.name: Deployment ID of the deployed model
parameters.description: Deployment ID of the deployed model

parameters.name: API version
parameters.description: API version

parameters.name: temperature
parameters.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. We generally recommend altering this or `top_p` but not both.

parameters.name: top_p
parameters.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered. We generally recommend altering this or `temperature` but not both.

parameters.name: stream
parameters.description: If set, partial message deltas will be sent, like in ChatGPT. Tokens will be sent as data-only server-sent events as they become available, with the stream terminated by a `data: [DONE]` message.

parameters.name: stop
parameters.description: Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens. The returned text will not contain the stop sequence.

parameters.name: max_tokens
parameters.description: The maximum number of tokens allowed for the generated answer. By default, the number of tokens the model can return will be (4096 - prompt tokens).

parameters.name: presence_penalty
parameters.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

parameters.name: frequency_penalty
parameters.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim.

parameters.name: logit_bias
parameters.description: Modify the likelihood of specified tokens appearing in the completion. Accepts a json object that maps tokens (specified by their token ID in the tokenizer) to an associated bias value from -100 to 100. Mathematically, the bias is added to the logits generated by the model prior to sampling. The exact effect will vary per model, but values between -1 and 1 should decrease or increase likelihood of selection; values like -100 or 100 should result in a ban or exclusive selection of the relevant token.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help Azure OpenAI to monitor and detect abuse.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: The role of the messages author.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: An array of content parts with a defined type, each can be of type `text`.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: A representation of configuration data for a single Azure OpenAI chat extension. This will be used by a chat completions request that should use Azure OpenAI chat extensions to augment the response behavior. The use of this configuration is compatible only with Azure OpenAI.

parameters.name: top_n_documents
parameters.description: The configured top number of documents to feature for the configured query.

parameters.name: in_scope
parameters.description: Whether queries should be restricted to use of indexed data.

parameters.name: strictness
parameters.description: The configured strictness of the search relevance filtering. The higher of strictness, the higher of the precision but lower recall of the answer.

parameters.name: role_information
parameters.description: Give the model instructions about how it should behave and any context it should reference when generating a response. You can describe the assistant's personality and tell it how to format responses. There's a 100 token limit for it, and it counts against the overall token limit.

parameters.name: index_name
parameters.description: The name of the index to use as available in the referenced Azure Search resource.

parameters.name: title_field
parameters.description: The name of the index field to use as a title.

parameters.name: url_field
parameters.description: The name of the index field to use as a URL.

parameters.name: filepath_field
parameters.description: The name of the index field to use as a filepath.

parameters.name: content_fields
parameters.description: The names of index fields that should be treated as content.

parameters.name: content_fields_separator
parameters.description: The separator pattern that content fields should use.

parameters.name: vector_fields
parameters.description: The names of fields that represent vector data.

parameters.name: query_type
parameters.description: The type of Azure Search retrieval query that should be executed when using it as an Azure OpenAI chat extension.

parameters.name: semantic_configuration
parameters.description: The additional semantic configuration for the query.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Search filter.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Represents the available sources Azure OpenAI On Your Data can use to configure vectorization of data for use with vector search.

parameters.name: deployment_name
parameters.description: Specifies the name of the model deployment to use for vectorization. This model deployment must be in the same Azure OpenAI resource, but On Your Data will use this model deployment via an internal call rather than a public one, which enables vector search even in private networks.

parameters.name: n
parameters.description: How many chat completion choices to generate for each input message.

parameters.name: seed
parameters.description: If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same `seed` and parameters should return the same result.Determinism is not guaranteed, and you should refer to the `system_fingerprint` response parameter to monitor changes in the backend.

parameters.name: logprobs
parameters.description: Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens or not. If true, returns the log probabilities of each output token returned in the `content` of `message`. This option is currently not available on the `gpt-4-vision-preview` model.

parameters.name: top_logprobs
parameters.description: An integer between 0 and 5 specifying the number of most likely tokens to return at each token position, each with an associated log probability. `logprobs` must be set to `true` if this parameter is used.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Setting to `json_object` enables JSON mode. This guarantees that the message the model generates is valid JSON.

ExtensionsChatCompletions_Create.displayName: Using extensions to create a completion for chat messages

parameters.name: Deployment ID of the deployed model
parameters.description: Deployment ID of the deployed model

parameters.name: Confirm Deployment ID of the deployed model
parameters.description: Confirm Deployment ID of the deployed model

parameters.name: API version
parameters.description: API version

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the message in the conversation.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: The role of the author of this message.

parameters.name: recipient
parameters.description: The recipient of the message in the format of <namespace>.<operation>. Present if and only if the recipient is tool.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: The contents of the message

parameters.name: end_turn
parameters.description: Whether the message ends the turn.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The data source type.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: The parameters to be used for the data source in runtime.

parameters.name: stop
parameters.description: Array minimum size of 1 and maximum of 4

parameters.name: max_tokens
parameters.description: The maximum number of tokens allowed for the generated answer. By default, the number of tokens the model can return will be (4096 - prompt tokens).

parameters.name: presence_penalty

The exact effect will vary per model, but values between -1 and 1 should decrease or increase likelihood of selection; values like -100 or 100 should result in a ban or exclusive selection of the relevant token.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help Azure OpenAI to monitor and detect abuse.

returns.raw:
The response of the extensions chat completions.
